extraction.one_line_summary: MATTHEWS Michael won stage 3 in Melfi after Jayco Alula and Trek-Segafredo aggressively reduced the sprint field over two late climbs, distancing McNulty, Cort, and Kämna unexpectedly.
tactical_patterns: Jayco Alula paced both third-category climbs specifically to drop sprinters while keeping Matthews fresh, revealing stage-hunting ambition.
Evenepoel contested intermediate bonus seconds aggressively despite holding pink, signaling unwillingness to cede time before mountain stages begin.
Pinot attacked twice for KOM points early in Giro, suggesting career farewell motivation overrides traditional domestique role.

rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: First Giro stage win since 2015 after surviving selective climbs suggests improved climbing form compared to recent years.
rider_signals.McNulty Brandon: negative: Dropped on minor third-category climbs despite GC ambitions reveals vulnerability before serious mountain stages arrive.
rider_signals.CORT Magnus: negative: Eliminated before sprint finish on terrain expected to suit him indicates poor current form.
rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: negative: Distanced on minor climbs when positioned as protected rider suggests tactical miscalculation or form deficit.
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: Recovered from midrace drop to contest sprint shows exceptional recovery capacity within single stage.
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: neutral: Required significant effort to rejoin after crash but survived, revealing crash vulnerability rather than climbing weakness.
